name: Peña Mea Summit Trail
name_original: Levinco a Peña Mea en el Sendero Histórico
description: Hike the challenging 9.1-mile Peña Mea Historic Trail to the summit for panoramic views and the iconic Ojo de Buey natural arch.
region_intro: Hiking around Aller offers diverse mountain terrain in Asturias, Spain. The region is characterized by significant elevation changes, with routes ascending to summits and traversing valleys. Hikers can expect varied landscapes, from forested sections to open mountain passes. The area provides opportunities for both challenging ascents and moderate trails through natural environments. Best hiking trails around Aller The most popular hiking route is Peña Mea Summit Trail, a 9.1 miles (14.6 km) trail that takes 6 hours 15 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain to a prominent summit. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cabañaquinta to Colla L'Ablanu, a moderate 9.8 miles (15.7 km) path. This trail includes significant ascents through mountainous terrain. Local hikers also love the Marmartín River – Fuente Las Gavilanceras loop from Felechosa, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through river valleys and forested areas, often completed in about 2 hours 42 minutes. Hiking in Aller is defined by mountain peaks, river valleys, and varied forest sections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate valley walks to difficult summit ascents. The routes in Aller are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 1300 hikers have used komoot to explore Aller's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Aller?
A: Aller offers nearly 20 distinct hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy walks through river valleys to challenging ascents to mountain peaks.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect when hiking in Aller?
A: Hiking in Aller is characterized by diverse mountain terrain. You'll find routes with significant elevation changes, traversing forested sections, river valleys, and open mountain passes. The landscape includes both challenging summit ascents and moderate trails through natural environments.

Q: Are there any easy hiking options in Aller?
A: Yes, Aller has several easy hiking trails. There are 3 routes specifically categorized as easy, offering less strenuous options for those looking for a more relaxed experience amidst the region's natural beauty.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Aller?
A: The hiking trails in Aller are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an impressive average rating of 4.6 stars from over 160 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the varied terrain, from river valleys to mountain peaks, and the opportunities for both challenging and moderate walks.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Aller?
A: Yes, Aller offers several circular routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31856642" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmartín River – Fuente Las Gavilanceras loop from Felechosa&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 8 km trail that takes you through river valleys and forested areas.

Q: Can I find trails that follow rivers in Aller?
A: Yes, the region features trails that follow river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40311669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Salud Spring – Marmartín River loop from La Carva&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that leads through such scenic areas, offering a different perspective of Aller's landscape.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or summits to reach on hikes in Aller?
A: Absolutely. Aller is known for its mountain peaks and offers several viewpoints. You can find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151883" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coto Bello Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1074608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urbiés Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. For those seeking a summit, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5236418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Llosorio&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5565301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Polio&lt;/a&gt; are popular destinations.

Q: What kind of mountain passes can I explore in Aller?
A: Aller is home to several significant mountain passes that offer challenging and scenic hiking experiences. Notable passes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5619300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Santo Emiliano&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3592919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Cordal&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3592968" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Colladiella&lt;/a&gt;, providing routes with substantial elevation changes.

Q: Are there any trails suitable for experienced hikers looking for a challenge?
A: Yes, Aller offers challenging routes for experienced hikers. There are 6 difficult trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048076316/pena-mea-summit-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Mea Summit Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 14.6 km route with over 1000 meters of elevation gain, leading to a prominent summit.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Aller?
A: Given the mountainous terrain and potential for significant elevation, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for hiking in Aller, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can also be suitable, especially for higher altitudes, but be prepared for warmer weather. Winter hiking may require specialized gear due to snow and ice at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any trails that offer a good balance of distance and elevation?
A: For a moderate challenge with a good balance of distance and elevation, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047549687/cabanaquinta-to-colla-l-ablanu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabañaquinta to Colla L'Ablanu&lt;/a&gt; route. This 15.7 km trail includes significant ascents through mountainous terrain, making it a popular choice among local hikers.

Q: What kind of natural features define the Aller hiking experience?
A: The hiking experience in Aller is defined by its dramatic natural features, including towering mountain peaks, serene river valleys, and varied forest sections. Hikers can expect to encounter diverse landscapes that showcase the rugged beauty of Asturias, Spain.
tips: The Ojo de Buey (L'Arcón de la Finistra) is in the Peña Mea massif, in central Asturias, between the councils of Aller and Laviana. It is an impressive window of more than 20 meters in diameter, created by erosion of the limestone rock. It is worth seeing this tremendous hole from different angles.
Peña Mea, with its 1557 m altitude, is one of the best known peaks in Asturias.

The Pelúgano pass is located at an altitude of 1,017 meters, in front of the Peña Mea massif. From here you can enjoy beautiful panoramic views of the limestone mountain range and the very green slopes dotted with trees.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of hiking to Peña Mea on the Historic Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered difficult. It involves significant elevation gain, technical sections, and can be hazardous, especially in adverse weather. Hikers should have good fitness, sure-footedness, and potentially alpine experience. The final stretch to the summit is authentically alpine.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: The komoot tour data estimates the duration at approximately 6 hours and 15 minutes of moving time. However, due to the challenging terrain and significant elevation, it's wise to allow extra time for breaks, enjoying the views, and navigating technical sections.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several spectacular features. You'll encounter the impressive natural arch known as Ojo de Buey (L'Arcu de la Finiesta), a geological icon. From the Peña Mea Summit, you're rewarded with an overwhelming 360-degree panoramic view of the central Asturian mountain range, and on clear days, even the Cantabrian Sea and the Picos de Europa.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly diverse and demanding. You'll traverse gravel, unpaved paths, and alpine sections. Expect steep slopes, particularly in areas like the Canal de las Cuevas. Some descents can be steep, slippery, and have loose scree, requiring careful footing.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: Routes to Peña Mea often start from villages such as Pelúgano (Aller) or Les Campes (Laviana). While specific parking details aren't provided, these villages typically offer parking options for hikers. It's advisable to check local information for the most convenient trailhead parking.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike to Peña Mea?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is generally during the warmer, drier months to avoid adverse conditions. However, it's crucial to check weather reports before any ascent, as the mountain can become dangerous with rain, wet ground, or fog. The diverse scenery, from beech groves to mountain meadows, is beautiful in spring and autumn.

faqs.question: Is the Hiking to Peña Mea on the Historic Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, many trails in this region are dog-friendly. However, given the trail's difficult rating, technical sections, and potential for loose scree, it may not be suitable for all dogs. If bringing a dog, ensure it is fit, well-behaved, and accustomed to challenging mountain terrain. Always keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that a permit or entrance fee is required to hike to Peña Mea. Access to the natural areas of Asturias is generally open, but it's always good practice to respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Sendero Histórico: Asturias and the Asturias Interior (Etapa 10). This means you might encounter markers or sections shared with these longer-distance routes, adding to the historical context of your hike.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Starting points like Pelúgano or Les Campes are villages that may offer basic amenities such as cafes or small pubs. These can be good spots for a pre-hike coffee or a post-hike refreshment. It's recommended to check opening hours, especially outside peak season.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support, appropriate layers of clothing for changing mountain weather, plenty of water and snacks, a map and compass or GPS device, and a first-aid kit. Trekking poles are highly recommended for stability on steep and technical sections.

waypoints.name: Ojo de Buey (L'Arcu de la Finiesta), Peña Mea
